comments.body:
I think yes, and it’s a small, reasonable branding change.

The active footer is configured in [`quartz.config.yaml`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/main/quartz.config.yaml#L279-L282): the Quartz footer plugin is enabled, but `links` is empty. The loader passes those options into the footer component from the registry in [`config-loader.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/literature-template/blob/main/quartz/plugins/loader/config-loader.ts#L582-L608).

One caveat: this is probably not just a YAML `links` change. The upstream footer plugin only exposes `links`, while the attribution sentence itself is rendered by the component as “Created with” + the Quartz link ([upstream `Footer.tsx`](https://github.com/quartz-community/footer/blob/main/src/components/Footer.tsx#L31-L36)). So adding a Sepo link would not make it say “Powered by Quartz and Sepo” jointly.

Recommended implementation:

1. Add a small local footer component/plugin that renders something like:
   `Powered by Quartz and Sepo © 2026`
2. Link `Quartz` to `https://quartz.jzhao.xyz/`.
3. Link `Sepo` to the canonical Sepo URL we want to use.
4. Register that local component as the footer before layout generation, preserving the existing Quartz layout path.
5. Run the normal build to verify the footer renders across page frames.

Only clarification I’d need before coding is the exact Sepo URL to use.

---
_Run: `codex` / `gpt-5.5/xhigh` / `high`_

Added a local Sepo footer component and registered it as the Quartz footer, rendering “Powered by Quartz and Sepo” with Sepo linked to https://self-evolving.app. Verified touched-file formatting, TypeScript no-emit, and a full Quartz build under Node 22.

title: Let's modify the footer such that it should say powered by quartz and sepo jointly?
